description: Merged UK residential energy tariff dataset. Regional rates are in pence per kWh (p/kWh) or pence per day (p/day), inclusive of VAT at 5% unless noted otherwise. Fields: elec_unit = electricity unit rate; elec_sc = electricity standing charge; e7_day/e7_night = Economy 7 day/night rates; e7_sc = Economy 7 standing charge; peak_unit/off_peak_unit = time-of-use peak/off-peak rates; gas_unit = gas unit rate; gas_sc = gas standing charge.

providers.name: Affect Energy
providers.note: Affect Energy was acquired by Octopus Energy. Existing customers were migrated to Octopus Energy. No new tariffs available.

providers.name: British Gas
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. British Gas uses 13 PES regions; South East England A/B are two sub-regions within the South East PES zone.

tariffs.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. British Gas uses 13 PES regions; South East England A/B are two sub-regions within the South East PES zone. Source: EnergyPlus.
All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. E7 rates not available per-region (range: day 29.2–35.9 p/kWh, night 11.4–12.9 p/kWh). South East England A/B are two sub-regions. Source: EnergyPlus.

All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Off-peak rate uniform at 9.00 p/kWh across all regions. South East England A/B are two sub-regions within the British Gas South East PES zone. Source: EnergyPlus.

providers.name: Bulb
providers.note: Acquired by Octopus Energy in December 2022. All Bulb customers moved to Octopus Energy.

providers.name: Ecotricity
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: Green Variable
tariffs.note: Green Variable is Ecotricity's standard variable tariff combining Green Electricity and Green Gas variable products. This tariff is derogated from the Ofgem price cap. Rates effective from 01 July 2026. Source: Ecotricity domestic tariffs PDF June 2026 edition.

tariffs.name: EcoFixed - 1 Year Fixed Tariff June 2026
tariffs.note: Only available through Uswitch or MONY Group websites. Smart meter required; if not already installed, must be fitted within 3 months of tariff start date. All prices inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: EcoFixed - 1 Year Fixed EV Tariff June 2026
tariffs.note: 5 hour off-peak window for EV owners. Prices inclusive of VAT. Smart meter mandatory; must be installed within 3 months of tariff start if not already in place. Source: Ecotricity domestic tariffs PDF June 2026 (effective 12 June 2026).

tariffs.name: EcoLoyalty - 2 Year Green Tariff April 2026
tariffs.note: Historic tariff — no longer available for sale as of June 2026. Economy 7 off-peak hours usually 00:00–07:00 GMT. Source: Ecotricity domestic tariffs PDF June 2026 (EcoLoyalty - 2 Year Electricity V1 04/26 and EcoLoyalty - 2 Year Gas V1 04/26).

tariffs.name: Smart Import/Export and Smart Export Tariff
tariffs.note: Smart Import/Export and Smart Export Tariff from Ecotricity is an electricity-only variable tariff combining import supply with Smart Export Guarantee (SEG) solar export at 16p/kWh. Requires SMETS1 or SMETS2 smart meter in Smart mode with half-hourly data consent. No fixed contract term, no exit fees. Regional unit rates and standing charges are not publicly listed on EnergyPlus or Ecotricity's public pages - rates are communicated in Welcome Packs. No regional pricing data could be extracted.

providers.name: E.ON Next
providers.note: Exact unit rates for some tariffs require a personalised quote from eonnext.com.

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Fixed v53
tariffs.note: Regional per-rate data not published on source page (https://www.energyplus.co.uk/suppliers/eon-next/tariffs). Only national approximate averages available: ~25.3p/kWh electricity, ~6.2p/kWh gas. Exact regional rates require postcode-based quote from E.ON Next directly.

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Pledge v2
tariffs.note: Tariff is guaranteed to remain £100/yr below the Ofgem default tariff cap for the 12-month term. Internally known as Next Pledge Tracker 12m V9 on EnergyPlus. The two South East England entries correspond to London/SE-A (lower standing charge, id 12) and South East England B (higher standing charge, id 19).

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Online
tariffs.note: Regional unit rates and standing charges are not published on the EnergyPlus tariff page. The tariff is described as tracking the Ofgem price cap exactly. No per-region p/kWh or p/day values are available from the source page.

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Drive
tariffs.note: Regional rate breakdown not published on EnergyPlus page. Off-peak rate 6.7p/kWh is a single national rate. Peak rates described as standard cap-aligned (~27–28p/kWh June 2026) but no per-region figures are provided. Standing charges are standard cap-aligned but not broken down by region. Postcode-level quote required via comparison form.

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Heat Pump v2
tariffs.note: The EnergyPlus source page (https://www.energyplus.co.uk/suppliers/eon-next/tariffs) describes E.ON Next Heat Pump v2 as a flat single-rate 14p/kWh electricity tariff for MCS-installed heat pumps only. No per-region breakdown is provided on the page — only a national flat rate with cap-aligned standing charge. No regional unit rates, standing charges, or peak/off-peak hours are available from this source.

tariffs.name: E.ON Next Export (Next Export)
tariffs.note: This is a Smart Export Guarantee (SEG) tariff paying 16.5p/kWh flat export rate. No regional breakdown is available — it is a single national flat export rate. No supply rates (unit, standing charge) apply as this is export-only. Smart meter required by SEG regulation.

providers.name: Good Energy
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Gas sc uniform at 34.05 p/day. East Midlands E7/gas unit rate missing from source PDF.
All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Gas sc uniform at 48.44 p/day.
All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Gas sc uniform at 29.11 p/day.

tariffs.tariff: Good Energy EV Charge 12m Fix Jun26
tariffs.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Off-peak rate uniform at 9.50 p/kWh all regions. Annual cost based on 5,600 kWh (50% off-peak).

tariffs.tariff: Good Energy Heat Pump 12m Fix Jun26
tariffs.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT. Off-peak rate uniform at 14.00 p/kWh all regions. Annual cost based on 7,000 kWh combined heat pump usage.

providers.name: Octopus Energy
providers.note: All prices inc. VAT. Bulb was acquired by Octopus in Dec 2022.

tariffs.name: Flexible Octopus
tariffs.note: London (id 12) not listed as a separate region on the source page. South East England mapped to id 19. Economy 7 rates included per region.

tariffs.name: Octopus 12M Fixed April 2026 v3
tariffs.note: Economy 7 rates available per region. Two South East England entries mapped as: lower standing charge (A) = London/id 12, higher (B) = South East England/id 19. Effective date inferred from tariff name (April 2026).

tariffs.name: Octopus Tracker v3
tariffs.note: Rates are daily-priced based on day-ahead wholesale market (N2EX GB Day-Ahead for electricity, Marex Spectron for gas). Unit rates shown are current as of 2026-06-19, fetched from Octopus Energy API product SILVER-26-04-01 (Octopus Tracker April 2026 v1). All rates include VAT at 5%. Cap: 100p/kWh electricity, 30p/kWh gas. 9-month rejoin restriction if leaving the tariff.

tariffs.name: Agile Octopus
tariffs.note: Half-hourly pricing tied to wholesale day-ahead market. Unit rates shown are standing charges only — actual unit rates vary every 30 minutes. Rates shown are the cap-aligned/reference rates from the Octopus API (AGILE-24-10-01). Smart meter in half-hourly settlement mode (SMETS2) required. Capped at 100p/kWh inc VAT.

tariffs.name: Intelligent Octopus Go
tariffs.note: Product code INTELLI-FIX-12M-26-06-13 (Intelligent Octopus Go 12M Fixed June 2026 v1). Off-peak rate of 7.99995 p/kWh is uniform across all regions. Peak rates vary by region. Off-peak window 22:30-04:30 UTC = 23:30-05:30 BST. Requires compatible smart EV charger or compatible vehicle. No gas tariff available.

tariffs.name: Cosy Octopus
tariffs.note: Three daily off-peak windows (9 hours total) designed for heat pumps. Rates are variable and change daily. Peak window 16:00-19:00. Off-peak rate is approx 51% below standard day rate; peak is approx 50% above. Data sourced from Octopus API product COSY-22-12-08 for 2026-06-19.

tariffs.name: Octopus Flux
tariffs.note: The EnergyPlus page for Octopus Flux (https://www.energyplus.co.uk/suppliers/Octopus-Energy/tariffs/Octopus-Flux) only shows Solar Export data for 1 PES region (Eastern England). The page does not display import time-of-use rates or Monthly Direct Debit payment method data. Payment method field is blank on the page. Only Eastern England has a rate shown (29.320 p/kWh export rate); no standing charge is listed. No peak/off-peak import rates are shown. Data extracted as available from the specified URL as of 18 Jun 2026.

tariffs.name: Outgoing Octopus
tariffs.note: Solar export tariff. Only Eastern England (1 PES region) listed with data on the page. No standing charge shown for export tariff.

providers.name: Outfox Energy
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: Fix'd Dual Jun26 24M v5
tariffs.note: South East England (1) mapped to id 12 (lower standing charge); South East England (2) mapped to id 19. Economy 7 rates available for all regions.

tariffs.name: Fix'd Elec May26 12M v5
tariffs.note: South East England split into two sub-regions by standing charge level: lower SC maps to London (id 12), higher SC maps to South East England (id 19). Effective date inferred from page update date. Smart meter requirement not disclosed on page.

tariffs.name: Fix'd Dual Nov25 12M v1.0
tariffs.note: South East England Option 1 (lower standing charge) mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A); South East England Option 2 mapped to id 19 (South East England B). Smart meter requirement not specified on tariff page.

tariffs.name: Fix'd Dual Jun26 24M v4
tariffs.note: Economy 7 rates available per region. South East England A (id 12) identified by lower standing charge (44.83 p/day); South East England B (id 19) by higher standing charge (54.45 p/day).

tariffs.name: 2-year Fix'd Dual Jul25 v5.0
tariffs.note: Economy 7 rates available per region. South East England Option 1 mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A), Option 2 mapped to id 19 (South East England B). Smart meter requirement not specified on page.

tariffs.name: Outfox Export
tariffs.note: Solar export tariff. Only Eastern England region shown on the page with a unit rate of 1.050 p/kWh. No standing charge applies (export tariff). No Economy 7 available. No gas rates.

providers.name: OVO Energy
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: Simpler Energy
tariffs.note: Multiple rows per region exist on the source page (likely different meter types). First Dual Fuel row selected per region. South East England split into id 12 (lower sc, 44.83p/day) and id 19 (higher sc, 54.45p/day) based on standing charge pattern. No explicit London region on page.

tariffs.name: OVO 2 Year Fixed
tariffs.note: The tariff detail page https://www.energyplus.co.uk/suppliers/ovo/tariffs/ovo-2-year-fixed returns HTTP 404 and does not exist. Only one region (London) was available via the OVO supplier page live rates snapshot. Full per-region data could not be extracted.

providers.name: ScottishPower
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: ScottishPower Standard
tariffs.note: London region (id 12) not separately listed on the tariff page. South East England mapped to id 19. 13 PES regions covered.

tariffs.name: Cap Tracker Sep 2026 S1
tariffs.note: South East England (1) mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A, lower standing charge). South East England (2) mapped to id 19 (South East England B). Economy 7 rates available per region.

tariffs.name: EV Saver Apr 2026
tariffs.note: Economy 7 structure. Off-peak hours not specified on source page. Dual fuel and electricity-only options available; gas rates not extracted per EV tariff schema rules. South East England (1) mapped to id 12 (lower SC 41.58p/day); South East England (2) mapped to id 19 (higher SC 57.83p/day).

tariffs.name: Heat Pump Saver Nov 2026
tariffs.note: Economy 7 based heat pump tariff. Off-peak hours not specified on source page. South East England A (id 12) has lower standing charge. Gas rates available but not extracted per heat pump tariff rules. Effective date inferred from tariff name (Nov 2026).

tariffs.name: Solar Saver May 2027 v1
tariffs.note: Only Economy 7 rates are listed for this tariff. Standard (non-E7) electricity unit rates are not shown. The two South East England rows are mapped to London (id 12, lower standing charge) and South East England B (id 19, higher standing charge). Smart meter requirement not stated on page.

providers.name: Shell Energy
providers.note: Shell Energy's retail energy business was acquired by Octopus Energy in 2023. Existing customers were migrated to Octopus Energy. No new tariffs available.

providers.name: So Energy
providers.note: All prices p/kWh or p/day, inclusive of VAT.

tariffs.name: So Flex
tariffs.note: Page shows 2 pricing variants per region under the same So Flex name. Values shown are for the first (lower unit rate) variant per region. South East England has 4 rows; rows with lower standing charges mapped to London (id 12) and South East England (id 19) per DNO zone convention. Economy 7 rates also extracted per region.

tariffs.name: So Green Tracker
tariffs.note: Tracker tariff with two pricing rows per region; first (lower) row used as current rate. South East England appears as four rows, split into London (id 12, lower standing charge) and South East England (id 19, higher standing charge). Economy 7 rates also available per region. Page updated 18 Jun 2026.

tariffs.name: So Cheetah 12m
tariffs.note: Economy 7 rates available per region. South East England (1) with lower standing charge mapped to id 12 (London/SE England A); South East England (2) mapped to id 19 (SE England B).

tariffs.name: So Alpaca 12m
tariffs.note: South East England entry with lower standing charge (37.29p/day) mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A); entry with higher standing charge (47.32p/day) mapped to id 19 (South East England B).

tariffs.name: So Alpaca 18m
tariffs.note: South East England appears as two entries: id 12 (London/South East A, lower standing charge 38.16p/day) and id 19 (South East England B, higher standing charge 49.73p/day). Smart meter requirement not stated on page. Effective date inferred from page update date of 18 Jun 2026.

tariffs.name: So Cheetah 24m
tariffs.note: South East England Option 1 (lower standing charge 39.88 p/day) mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A). South East England Option 2 (higher standing charge 51.47 p/day) mapped to id 19 (South East England B).

tariffs.name: So Hampstead EV
tariffs.note: Off-peak hours not specified on page. Smart meter requirement not stated. Two South East England regions distinguished by standing charge: lower (51.38p/day) mapped to id 12 (London/South East England A), higher (57.03p/day) mapped to id 19 (South East England B). Tariff shows Economy 7 peak/off-peak rates only — no standard single-rate electricity available.

tariffs.name: So Aspen Two Year – Green
tariffs.note: Economy 7 rates available per region. South East England (lower standing charge) mapped to London/id 12; South East England (higher standing charge) mapped to id 19.

providers.name: Utility Warehouse
providers.note: Prices inc. 5% VAT (source PDFs are ex-VAT; 1.05 multiplier applied).

tariffs.name: UW Variable Gold
tariffs.note: Prices in source PDF are excluding VAT. All values here have been multiplied by 1.05 to include 5% VAT. Document reference: UWETL010426. E7 standing charges per region are as listed under Economy 7 Daily Standing Charge column.

tariffs.name: UW Fixed Saver 88
tariffs.note: Prices are ex-VAT in source; all rates here are inc. 5% VAT. Dual fuel discount of £12.38 ex. VAT (£12.999 inc. VAT) per fuel per year is deducted daily from standing charge for customers taking both gas and electricity. E7 standing charge is listed separately in source (not same as general domestic elec_sc). Tariff requires a 3 or 4 service bundle with Utility Warehouse.

tariffs.name: UW Fixed Start 88
tariffs.note: Prices converted to inc. VAT at 5%. Dual fuel discount of £12.38 ex. VAT per fuel deducted daily from standing charge for customers taking both gas and electricity. Economy 7 standing charges listed separately from standard electricity standing charges.

tariffs.name: UW Variable EV Value
tariffs.note: Prices in source PDF are stated as excluding VAT; all values here are inclusive of 5% VAT. Smart meter in credit mode required. Electric Vehicle (Category B battery or plug-in hybrid) required. Fair use policy applies (max 3 EVs, must be registered to supply address). Code: UWETL010426.

tariffs.name: UW Variable EV Gold
tariffs.note: Prices are VAT-inclusive at 5%. EV tariff requires a Category B battery EV or plug-in hybrid registered at the supply address, plus a smart meter in credit mode. Available to customers on a 2-service bundle with UW (Gold tier). Dual fuel discount of £13.00 inc. VAT per fuel deducted daily from the standing charge where applicable. Fair use policy applies (max 3 EVs).

tariffs.name: UW Tracker Saver 1
tariffs.note: Prices are excluding VAT per the PDF; all values here are VAT-inclusive at 5%. Tariff only available to customers taking a three or four service bundle. Prices track the Government's price cap at a set discount and may change each time the price cap is updated.

tariffs.name: UW Tracker Plus 1
tariffs.note: Prices are excluding VAT per the PDF; all values here have had 5% VAT applied. Tariff tracks the Government's price cap at a set discount. Requires a two-service bundle. Tracker end date 30 June 2027. Exit fee of £75 (inc. VAT) per fuel applies within tracker term.
